source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A square piece of paper is cut into six rectangular pieces as shown on the right. The sum of the perimeters of the six pieces is $120 \mathrm{~cm}$. How big is the area of the square?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown on the right', so the cut layout is required
the task depends on the geometry of how the square is partitioned into six rectangles, which is not specified in text alone
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires using the diagram structure together with the given total perimeter value
the target is the area of the original square, so the solver must infer a side-length-related quantity before computing area
this is not a direct visual lookup; it involves geometric reasoning over a partitioned figure
the problem is multiple-choice, but the underlying reasoning still appears to require more than one step
verifiability: answer_text is a single exact numeric result with units: '$144 \\mathrm{~cm}^{2}$'
answer_type is short_text, which is straightforward to normalize and check
choices are provided and one option matches the stated answer
the geometry setup plus a numeric final answer makes downstream rule-based or symbolic checking feasible
asset registry passed and both question and answer are present

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no reported issues
core assets are complete: question text, required image, and answer are all present
the image is required for reasoning, but the image file exists and is available
initial scoring is strong, especially on image dependency and verifiability
the answer is a short exact numeric result, which supports reliable downstream checking
